E-01.solution: Turn off and on again. Check for paper or protective material inside.

E-02.solution: Turn the printer off and then back on again.

E-10.meaning: Waste ink pad at end of service life
E-10.solution: Ink pad needs replacement by an authorized Epson service provider.

E-11.solution: Contact Epson or an authorized service provider. Not user-serviceable.

E-12.meaning: Borderless printing ink pad needs replacement
E-12.solution: Contact Epson. Standard printing may still work.

E-13.meaning: Ink pad reached end of service life
E-13.solution: Contact Epson Support to replace the ink pad.

0x97.solution: Power cycle. If persists, professional servicing required.

0xF1.solution: Realign the printhead or reseat the ink cartridges.

0xFA.meaning: Ink cartridge error (not seated or incompatible)
0xFA.solution: Reseat or replace with a genuine Epson cartridge.

W-01.solution: Remove jammed paper and press the button on the LCD to clear the error.

W-11.meaning: Ink pad nearing end of service life
W-11.solution: Contact Epson. Printing can usually continue by pressing start.

W-14.meaning: Borderless printing ink pad nearing end of life
W-14.solution: Contact Epson. You can resume printing with borders by pressing start.

W-15.meaning: Ink pad nearing end of service life

I-22.meaning: Setting up Wi-Fi using WPS Push Button
I-22.solution: Push the WPS button on your router.

I-23.solution: Enter the PIN shown on the printer's LCD into your router within 2 minutes.

I-31.meaning: Setting up Wi-Fi from a computer
I-31.solution: Run the printer software installer on your computer.

I-41.meaning: Paper Setup Auto Display is disabled
I-41.solution: Enable this setting in the printer's menu to use AirPrint.

Cannot Print.meaning: Mechanical problem or foreign object inside
Cannot Print.solution: Open machine, remove foreign objects. Disconnect power for several minutes, reconnect.

Clean Drum.solution: Clean the drum unit per your printer manual.

Cover Open.meaning: A cover is not properly closed
Cover Open.solution: Lift and firmly close the indicated cover until it locks.

Ink Absorber Full.solution: Requires replacement. Contact Brother customer service.

Jam Front.solution: Gently remove jammed paper. Check paper guides are set correctly.

Jam Rear.solution: Gently remove jammed paper from rear area.

No Paper Fed.meaning: Out of paper or paper not loaded correctly
No Paper Fed.solution: Refill or reload paper in the tray, ensuring it is centered.

System Error.solution: Turn off and on again. If persists, contact Brother Customer Service.

30-3F.solution: Clear the jammed paper from the printer.

40.solution: Turn off and unplug. Allow to cool down, then retry.

46.solution: Contact Brother customer service for replacement.

50-5E.solution: Clear paper jams first. If persists, contact Brother support.

71.solution: Check firmware, power cycle, print a report. If returns, contact Brother.

7D.solution: Clean the printer drum per your manual.

CF.solution: Empty waste toner container carefully, avoid spillage.

E51.solution: Power cycle. If persists, laser unit may need professional replacement.

E52.solution: Power cycle. If persists, contact support.

26.solution: Remove cartridges, reset printer (off/on), reinstall firmly.

27.solution: Remove cartridges, reset printer (off/on), reinstall firmly.

28.solution: Remove cartridges, reset printer (off/on), reinstall firmly.
